metadata.title: Munin — the customer platform for the agentic era
metadata.description: Open-source headless business app suite (Knowledge Base, Conversations, CRM, CMS) where the AI agent is the UI.

common.networkError: Network error — is the API reachable?

home.title: The open source customer platform for the <accent>agentic</accent> era.
home.subtitle: CRM, knowledge, content, outreach, and conversations in one MCP-first system. Agents work against the same tools and data as your team — with review and handover where it matters.

invalid.title: That email and password don't match.
invalid.hint: Check the address, or try again.
invalid.hintWithReset: Check the address, or <resetLink>reset your password</resetLink>.

unreachable.title: Couldn't reach the server.
unreachable.hint: Check your connection and try again.

signUp.title: Create your account
signUp.subtitle: The customer platform for the agentic era.
signUp.invitationInvalid: This invitation is no longer valid (revoked or expired).
signUp.invitationLookupFailed: Could not look up the invitation.

login.quote: Huginn and Muninn fly each day over the wide world. I fear for Huginn, that he come not back, yet more anxious am I for Muninn.

loginError.quote: The raven knows your face, not your password. Try again — or send word, and a new key shall come.

signup.quote: Memory is older than thought. Both ravens fly, but only one always returns.

forgot.quote: Memory falters, but the raven returns. Send word, and a path will open again.

reset.quote: What is forgotten can be set anew. The flock keeps the door, but you keep the key.

invite.quote: Two ravens are better than one. The flock remembers what no single mind can hold.

inviteBad.quote: Even Muninn forgets, when too many days have passed. Ask again, and the flock will remember.

forgotPassword.title: Reset your password
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your account email and we'll send you a reset link.
forgotPassword.sent: If an account exists for that email, we've sent a reset link.
forgotPassword.failed: Could not start the reset flow.
forgotPassword.sentBody: We sent a reset link to {email}. It's good for the next 30 minutes.
forgotPassword.sentInfo: Didn't get it? Check spam, or wait a minute and try again. The link expires after one use.

resetPassword.title: Pick a new password
resetPassword.subtitle: For {email}. At least 10 characters.
resetPassword.success: Password updated. You can sign in now.
resetPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
resetPassword.missingToken: This reset link is missing or invalid. Request a new one.
resetPassword.doneSubtitle: You're all set. Sign in with your new password to continue.

verifyEmail.successSubtitle: Your account is ready. Sign in to continue.
verifyEmail.expiredSubtitle: Verification links are valid for 24 hours. Sign in to receive a new one.
verifyEmail.invalidSubtitle: We didn't recognize this verification link. Sign in to request a new one.

runnerStatusBanner.agentOfflineMessage: The in-process agent runner can't reach its AI provider — {reason}. Drafts and auto-replies are paused until credentials are restored.
runnerStatusBanner.providerErrorRegional: provider region not enabled — check provider account or change provider
runnerStatusBanner.providerErrorRateLimit: provider rate limit hit — likely a plan or billing issue
runnerStatusBanner.providerErrorModelNotFound: selected model not available — pick a different model

saveFailed.title: Save failed
saveFailed.sub: Nothing was saved · safe to retry
saveFailed.body: Munin couldn't reach the server. Your inputs are still here — try again, or come back in a moment.

settings.title: The connection broke.
settings.lede: We couldn't reach Munin to load this page. Your data is safe — this is a transport problem, not a loss.

inbox.title: The flock did not return.
inbox.lede: We couldn't reach Munin to load your inbox. Your data is safe — this is a transport problem, not a loss.

overview.title: Word from the flock.
overview.ledeQuiet: All quiet. Drafts and conversations land here as they arrive.
overview.ledeLiveOnly: {count, plural, =1 {# live conversation is paused on you. Agents are caught up otherwise.} other {# live conversations are paused on you. Agents are caught up otherwise.}}
overview.ledeQueueOnly: {count, plural, =1 {# draft waiting. Agents are caught up otherwise.} other {# drafts waiting. Agents are caught up otherwise.}}
overview.ledeBoth: {live, plural, =1 {# live conversation is paused on you} other {# live conversations are paused on you}}, {queue, plural, =1 {# more draft waiting} other {# more drafts waiting}}. Agents are caught up otherwise.

empty.title: An empty perch.
empty.body: No drafts yet. Connect an agent and Munin will start collecting their work here — drafts to approve, conversations to take over, knowledge to confirm.

drawer.subject: Subject — {subject}
drawer.cmsCoverDropHint: Drop an image or click to upload — 1600×900
drawer.cmsScheduleDescription: Pick when this draft should go live. Munin will publish it automatically at that time — no further approval needed.
drawer.cmsScheduleError: Pick a time in the future.
drawer.feedbackApproveConfirm: Approve sends the title and body above to Munin developers.
drawer.crmMergeExplain: Keeps the primary; rewrites foreign keys; archives the loser. No conversations are lost.

activity.title: From the perch.
activity.subtitle: Every realtime event firing on this org, as it happens. Streamed from the same channel the dashboards read from.

agents.title: The flock.
agents.subtitle: Every OAuth-authorized agent connected to this org. Revoke one to immediately cut off its MCP access.
agents.emptyBody: Add the MCP URL to Claude Desktop, Cursor, or your runtime, then complete consent — connected agents will appear here.

channels.title: Where the flock fly.
channels.subtitle: Inbound surfaces where end-users reach you.
channels.createWidgetDescription: Mints a `mn_widget_*` API key bound to this channel. The widget loads on the configured origins and posts via /v1/widget/messages.
channels.nameHint: For your reference. Lowercase, no spaces.
channels.originsHint: Comma- or whitespace-separated origins (full URLs with scheme + host) that may load the widget.
channels.originsInvalid: Not a valid URL: {invalid}. Each origin must include the scheme + host, e.g. https://example.com.
channels.createdTitle: Channel created — copy these secrets now
channels.createdDescription: Two values for {name}. We store both server-side; the widget key is shown only once on creation, the identity secret only on creation and rotation.
channels.identitySecretHint: Keep this on your server. Use it to compute data-user-hash for verified visitors. Never expose it in the browser.
channels.rotateConfirm: Rotate the widget key for {name}. Existing keys will be revoked.
channels.rotatedDescription: New widget key for {name}. Update your widget config and dispatch.
channels.rotateIdentityConfirm: Rotate the identity secret for {name}. Previously-issued user hashes will stop verifying immediately and you'll need to re-render every page that uses data-user-hash.
channels.rotatedIdentityTitle: Identity secret rotated — copy it now
channels.rotatedIdentityDescription: New identity verification secret for {name}. Re-deploy your hash-computing code with the new value.

embed.title: Embed snippet — {name}
embed.description: Paste this on every page where you want the widget to appear.
embed.scriptHint: Replace [your widget key] with the mn_widget_* shown when this channel was created. To identify a known visitor, add data-external-id and data-user-hash (see below). For theming, positioning, and other options, see the widget guide.
embed.hashHint: If you want to bind a chat to a known user (e.g. so you can resume their thread next visit), compute a server-side HMAC with the channel's identity secret and pass it as data-user-hash:

channels.emptyBody: Add a chat widget or email channel to start collecting messages.

sendTest.title: Send a test email
sendTest.description: Send a real message through {name}'s outbound transport to confirm credentials and deliverability.
sendTest.toHint: Defaults to your account email — change it to send the test somewhere else.
sendTest.success: Test email queued for {to}. Check the inbox in a moment.

email.createDescription: Bring your own SMTP credentials (and optionally IMAP for inbound replies). Passwords are encrypted server-side; we never show them again.
email.fromAddressInvalid: Enter a complete email address like name@example.com.
email.portInvalid: Port must be a whole number between 1 and 65535.
email.hostInvalid: Host must be a fully-qualified hostname like smtp.example.com, an IP address, or localhost — no scheme, slashes, or spaces.
email.trackOpensHint: Embeds a 1×1 tracking pixel in the HTML part. Best-effort — many clients block images, Apple Mail Privacy Protection pre-loads them.
email.editDescription: Update the channel's name or transport. Leave a password blank to keep the stored credential — type a new one to replace it.
email.sendLimitsHelp: Optional caps on outbound email volume to protect domain reputation. Messages over the limit stay queued and send automatically when capacity opens. Leave blank for no limit.

addSmsDialog.title: Add an SMS channel
addSmsDialog.description: Pick a vendor and configure credentials. SMS arrives at the same generic webhook regardless of vendor — Munin verifies each provider's signature before ingesting.

addVoiceDialog.title: Add a voice channel
addVoiceDialog.description: Pick a voice AI vendor and connect credentials. The vendor runs the call end-to-end (STT, LLM, TTS); Munin receives transcripts and call artifacts via the shared webhook.

twilioSms.editDescription: Update name, Account SID, or sender. Leave Auth Token blank to keep the stored credential — type a new one to rotate.
twilioSms.nameHint: For your reference. Lowercase, no spaces.
twilioSms.accountSidHint: Starts with "AC". Found in the Twilio console under Account → API keys & tokens.
twilioSms.authTokenHintCreate: From the Twilio console. Encrypted at rest.
twilioSms.authTokenHintEdit: Leave blank to keep the existing token. Paste a new value to rotate.
twilioSms.fromNumberHint: E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567). The Twilio number SMS will be sent from.
twilioSms.messagingServiceSidHint: Starts with "MG". Use this instead of From number if you have a Messaging Service.
twilioSms.fromOrServiceHint: Either From number or Messaging Service SID is required — providing both is allowed.

sendTestDialog.title: Send a test SMS
sendTestDialog.description: Send a real SMS through {name} to confirm credentials and deliverability.
sendTestDialog.toHint: E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567). On a Twilio trial account, only verified caller IDs can receive.
sendTestDialog.bodyHint: Up to 1600 chars. Leave blank to send a default test message.
sendTestDialog.bodyPlaceholder: Munin test message — outbound SMS is working.

messageBirdSms.editDescription: Update name, originator, or rotate keys. Leave an encrypted field blank to keep the stored value.
messageBirdSms.nameHint: For your reference. Lowercase, no spaces.
messageBirdSms.accessKeyHintCreate: Live or test Access Key from your MessageBird Developers dashboard.
messageBirdSms.accessKeyHintEdit: Leave blank to keep the existing key. Paste a new value to rotate.
messageBirdSms.signingKeyHintCreate: Webhook signing key — used to verify the JWT on incoming SMS. Found in the MessageBird Developers dashboard.
messageBirdSms.signingKeyHintEdit: Leave blank to keep the existing key. Paste a new value to rotate.
messageBirdSms.originatorHint: An E.164 number registered with MessageBird (e.g. +15551234567) or an alphanumeric sender ID up to 11 characters (e.g. AcmeSupport). Alphanumeric senders cannot receive replies.

sendTestDialog.title: Send a test SMS
sendTestDialog.description: Send a real SMS through {name} via MessageBird to confirm credentials and deliverability.
sendTestDialog.bodyHint: Up to 1600 chars. Leave blank to send a default test message.
sendTestDialog.bodyPlaceholder: Munin test message — outbound SMS is working.

vapi.editDescription: Update name, IDs, or rotate keys. Leave a secret field blank to keep the stored value.
vapi.nameHint: For your reference. Lowercase, no spaces.
vapi.apiKeyHintCreate: Vapi private API key — used server-side to place outbound calls and manage the assistant. Never exposed to the browser.
vapi.apiKeyHintEdit: Leave blank to keep the existing key. Paste a new value to rotate.
vapi.webhookSecretHintCreate: Auto-generated. Copy it and paste it into the Vapi assistant's Advanced → Webhook Server → HTTP Headers as `X-Webhook-Secret`.
vapi.webhookSecretHintEdit: Click Generate new to rotate. Update the `X-Webhook-Secret` header in Vapi to match.

connectionStage.title: Configure Vapi
connectionStage.description: Paste these values into the Vapi assistant's Advanced → Webhook Server section, then click Done.
connectionStage.webhookSecretHint: Add as HTTP Header named `X-Webhook-Secret` with this value.

vapi.assistantIdHint: The Vapi assistant that handles calls on this channel.

assistantStage.title: Choose an assistant
assistantStage.description: Select the Vapi assistant that will handle calls on this channel.
assistantStage.empty: No assistants found in this Vapi account. Create one in Vapi, then try again.

replaceWebhook.title: Replace assistant server URL?
replaceWebhook.message: This Vapi assistant already has a server URL configured. Munin will overwrite it to receive call events. Continue?

vapi.phoneNumberIdHint: Only needed for phone network calls. Paste a phone number id from Vapi.
vapi.publicKeyHintCreate: Required for in-browser voice calls from the chat widget. Find it in the Vapi dashboard under your account/keys — it's safe to expose to the browser.
vapi.publicKeyHintEdit: Required for in-browser voice calls from the chat widget. Find it in the Vapi dashboard under your account/keys — it's safe to expose to the browser.

placeCallDialog.title: Place an outbound call
placeCallDialog.description: Start an outbound voice call through {name}. The Vapi assistant runs the conversation end-to-end.
placeCallDialog.customerNameHint: Shown in Vapi's call records and available to the assistant as a variable.

threll.editDescription: Update name, API key, or IDs. Leave the API key blank to keep the stored value.
threll.nameHint: For your reference. Lowercase, no spaces.
threll.apiKeyHintCreate: Threll API key (Settings → Developer). Munin uses it to register the webhook with Threll automatically.
threll.apiKeyHintEdit: Leave blank to keep the existing key. Paste a new value to rotate.
threll.workerHint: The threll that handles calls on this channel.

workerStage.title: Choose a threll
workerStage.description: Select the threll that will handle calls on this channel.
workerStage.descriptionAccount: Select the threll in {account} that will handle calls on this channel.
workerStage.empty: No thrells found in this account. Create one in Threll, then try again.

replaceWebhook.title: Replace existing webhook?
replaceWebhook.message: This Threll account already has an account-wide webhook subscription. Munin will delete it and register its own. Continue?

placeCallDialog.title: Place an outbound call
placeCallDialog.description: Start an outbound voice call through {name}. The Threll worker runs the conversation end-to-end.
placeCallDialog.customerNameHint: Shown in Threll's call records and available to the worker as a variable.

errors.createTwilioSms: Could not create Twilio SMS channel.
errors.updateTwilioSms: Could not update Twilio SMS channel.
errors.createMessageBirdSms: Could not create MessageBird SMS channel.
errors.updateMessageBirdSms: Could not update MessageBird SMS channel.
errors.sendTestMessageBirdSms: Could not send test SMS via MessageBird.
errors.createVapi: Could not create Vapi voice channel.
errors.updateVapi: Could not update Vapi voice channel.
errors.createThrell: Could not create Threll voice channel.
errors.updateThrell: Could not update Threll voice channel.

trackers.title: What people actually read.
trackers.subtitle: Track page views across your sites and apps.
trackers.createDescription: Mints a public `mn_track_*` key plus a server-side identity secret. The script loads on the configured origins and posts to /v1/a/t.
trackers.nameHint: For your reference. Lowercase, no spaces.
trackers.originsHint: Comma- or whitespace-separated origins (full URLs with scheme + host).
trackers.originsInvalid: Not a valid URL: {invalid}. Each origin must include the scheme + host, e.g. https://example.com.
trackers.createdTitle: Tracker created — copy these secrets now
trackers.createdDescription: Two values for {name}. The tracker key is shown only once on creation, the identity secret only on creation and rotation.
trackers.identitySecretHint: Keep this on your server. Use it to compute data-user-hash for verified visitors. Never expose it in the browser.
trackers.rotateKeyConfirm: Rotate the tracker key for {name}. The old mn_track_* key will be revoked; pages still embedding it will silently stop recording views.
trackers.rotatedKeyTitle: Tracker key rotated — copy it now
trackers.rotatedKeyDescription: New tracker key for {name}. Update every page that embeds the old key.
trackers.rotateIdentityConfirm: Rotate the identity secret for {name}. Previously-issued user hashes will stop verifying immediately.
trackers.rotatedIdentityTitle: Identity secret rotated — copy it now
trackers.rotatedIdentityDescription: New identity verification secret for {name}. Re-deploy your hash-computing code with the new value.
trackers.revokeConfirm: Revoke the public key for {name}. Pages still embedding this key will silently stop recording views.

embed.title: Embed snippet — {name}
embed.description: Paste this on every page where you want page views recorded.
embed.scriptHint: Replace [your tracker key] with the mn_track_* shown when this tracker was created. To identify a known visitor, add data-external-id and data-user-hash (see below).
embed.hashHint: Compute a server-side HMAC with this tracker's identity secret and pass it as data-user-hash. Page-views from that visitor will be stamped with their end_user_id. For SPAs where the user logs in after script load, call window.mn.identify(externalId, userHash) once the user is known.

trackers.emptyBody: Add a tracker to start collecting page-view analytics.

apiKeys.title: Keys to the gate.
apiKeys.subtitle: Admin keys for the Munin API. Each key is shown in plaintext exactly once — store it somewhere safe before closing the dialog.
apiKeys.mintModalSub: Plaintext shown once · cannot be retrieved later
apiKeys.nameHint: For your reference only — not sent to the API.
apiKeys.revealSub: Copy now — this is the only time you'll see it
apiKeys.revealWarning: Heads up. Munin stores only a hash of this key. Once you close this dialog, the plaintext is gone forever — copy it to your secret manager now.
apiKeys.createDescription: The plaintext value is shown ONCE, immediately after creation. Copy it now and store it in your secret manager.
apiKeys.emptyBody: Create your first key above to get started.
apiKeys.createdDescription: Munin only stores the hash. We can't show you this key again.

oauthConsent.title: Authorize <client></client>?
oauthConsent.lede: <client></client> wants to connect to Munin and act through the MCP toolset on your behalf. Review what it can reach before you decide.
oauthConsent.trustFirst: First time you're connecting <client></client> to this account. Nothing has been shared with it yet.
oauthConsent.scopesCount: {modules, plural, =0 {0 modules} =1 {1 module} other {# modules}} · {scopes, plural, =0 {0 scopes} =1 {1 scope} other {# scopes}}
oauthConsent.noModuleScopes: Sign-in only — no product permissions requested.

kb.readWrite: Read and edit your articles, spaces, and search.
kb.read: Read your articles, spaces, and search.

conv.readWrite: Read threads and send messages on your behalf.

crm.readWrite: Read and modify contact records, deals, and segments.
crm.read: Read contact records, deals, and segments.

cms.readWrite: Create, edit, and publish CMS content and schedules.
cms.read: Read CMS collections, entries, and assets.

outreach.readWrite: Draft campaigns and send to consented contacts. Consent checks stay enforced.

analytics.readWrite: Read analytics and manage tracker keys.
analytics.read: Read analytics for content and search engagement.

reassurance.body: <client></client> acts as <user></user> — it inherits your role and can't exceed it. It can't manage the team, billing, or API keys, or delete the org. Access lasts 90 days, auto-refreshed while in use, and you can revoke it anytime from <settings>Settings → Agents</settings>.

granted.title: Access granted.
granted.sub: can now act in Munin within the scopes you approved. Sending you back to the app.
granted.panelBody: An authorization code was issued and is being exchanged for a token. now holds the scopes you approved for Munin, valid for 90 days and refreshed silently.

denied.title: Access denied.
denied.sub: No token was issued. can't reach anything in Munin. Returning you to the app.
denied.panelBody: You denied the request, so no token was issued. You can close this window or start over from <client></client>.

oauthConsent.missingParams: Missing OAuth request. Return to the application that started the authorization flow.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

team.title: The council.
team.subtitle: Invite teammates and manage what they can see.
team.inviteDescription: They'll get an email with a link to accept. The link expires in 7 days.
team.manualShareBody: Set MUNIN_MAIL_PROVIDER (e.g. resend) and restart so future invites mail automatically.
team.removeConfirm: They lose access to the org immediately.
team.revokeInviteConfirm: The link sent to {email} stops working immediately. You can re-invite them later.
team.invitesEmptyBody: Use the button above to invite someone — they'll appear here until accepted.
team.inviteModalSub: Sends a sign-in link, expires in 7 days
team.inviteModalRoleHint: Members can read inbox + reply. Admins also manage channels and keys.
team.editNameSubtitle: Update the display name shown across Munin.

endUsers.title: Everyone you've talked to.
endUsers.subtitle: Every person on the other side of a Munin conversation — inbound or outbound, across email and the chat widget. Scoped per-org; never visible to other workspaces.
endUsers.emptyBody: Mint your first delegated end-user token via POST /v1/tokens/delegated with an admin key from your backend, and the corresponding EndUser will appear here.
endUsers.noTokensRevoked: No active tokens to revoke for this end-user.

getStarted.title: Wake the flock.
getStarted.lede: Connect your favourite model to Munin over MCP, then pick a recipe to wire up an agent that drops drafts into this inbox.
getStarted.apiKeyHint: Mint an API key in <link>Settings · API keys</link> and paste it in place of the placeholder. The token scopes the agent to a single org.

usage.title: The flock by the numbers.
usage.subtitle: MCP call, API call, and conversation rollups across all agents in this org. Sparklines show the last 14 days.

byAgent.title: By agent
byAgent.empty: No agent activity in this window.

auditLog.title: Munin's record.
auditLog.subtitle: Append-only trail of every administrative action. You cannot edit or delete entries; export them with the workspace export tool.
auditLog.filterDescription: Leave blank to match all values.
auditLog.emptyBody: Tool calls and control-plane writes appear here. Connect an agent and try a tool.

profile.title: Edit your details
profile.subtitle: Update the name shown on your account.

account.title: Your perch.
account.subtitle: Settings that apply to the whole organization.
account.orgNameHint: Shown in the topbar and on shared pages. Up to 128 characters.

agentSetup.lede: Name your workspace, pick an LLM provider, paste an API key, and choose models for chat and curation. The agent comes online as soon as you save.

org.title: Name your workspace
org.lede: Shown in the topbar and on shared pages. You can change this later in settings.

settings.title: Brief the raven.
settings.lede: Everything that thinks on your behalf — the persona, the model provider, and every job the AI handles, from chat replies to outreach drafts.

wizard.readyLede: From here, Munin handles new conversations on its own. The inbox shows what it's doing.

provider.title: Provider
provider.lede: Connect any OpenAI-compatible provider with your own key.

apiKey.title: API key
apiKey.ledeStored: An API key is stored. Paste a new one to replace it.
apiKey.ledeMissing: Paste your provider API key. Stored encrypted at rest.
apiKey.label: Key

connection.testUnsupported: Connected, but the provider doesn't expose /v1/models. Enter model ids manually.

models.title: Models
models.smartHint: Munin runs two models. Pick a fast, cheap one for chat-bot replies and quick cleanup; pick a stronger one for jobs that need judgment.
models.needKey: Save an API key to load the model list.
models.unsupported: Provider didn't return a model list. Enter model ids manually (e.g. anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5).
models.fastHint: Used for: chat-bot replies, signature stripping, classification, short summarization. Latency and cost matter more than smarts here.
models.smartUseHint: Used for: KB curation, CRM hygiene, outreach drafts — anywhere the agent needs to weigh judgment over many messages.

websiteImport.title: Seed from your website
websiteImport.lede: Optional: paste your public website URL and we'll read a few key pages — home, about, pricing, FAQ — into the knowledge base so the widget can answer real questions on day one.
websiteImport.urlHint: Public marketing site. We read up to 25 pages, no JavaScript rendering. Takes about a minute.

errors.enqueue: Couldn't start the import. Please check the URL and try again.

status.reading: Reading {host} — usually under a minute. You can leave this page; we'll keep going in the background.
status.stillRunning: Still working. It's taking longer than usual; check back in a minute.
status.doneSummary: Imported {count, plural, one {# page} other {# pages}} from {host} in {duration}s. Munin will use them to ground its answers.
status.doneSummaryNoTime: Imported {count, plural, one {# page} other {# pages}} from {host}. Munin will use them to ground its answers.
status.doneEmpty: We read {host} but didn't find pages to import.
status.failedSummary: Couldn't reach {host}. No pages were imported. Your agent is still live — import later from Settings.

persona.title: Persona
persona.blurb: Used wherever the AI speaks or writes to your customers — chat replies, outreach drafts, outreach replies.

models.title: Models
models.blurb: Model provider and credentials. Used by every job below.

conversational.title: Conversational
conversational.blurb: Speaks to your customers directly, in the configured persona.

background.neverRan: Never run for this org yet.

aiDriven.title: AI-driven
aiDriven.blurb: Run silently. The AI decides what to do using the same model as the chat assistant.

tasks.title: Scheduled tasks
tasks.blurb: Deterministic background jobs. May call the model for a single step, but the flow is fixed.

chatAssistant.title: Chat assistant
chatAssistant.description: Replies to customers across chat, email, and any other conversation channel.

identity.title: Name
identity.blurb: What the AI calls itself. Injected as a name preamble in every system prompt the AI uses to write to customers.
identity.nameHelp: Leave blank to omit a name. The AI will still talk in your brand voice; it just won't introduce itself by name.

acceptInvite.acceptedBody: You're now a member of the org.

errors.lookup: Could not look up the invitation.
errors.expired: This invitation is no longer valid (revoked or expired).

errors.SIGNUP_NOT_ALLOWED: Signup is restricted on this Munin instance. Ask an admin to invite you.
errors.SIGNUP_DOMAIN_NOT_ALLOWED: Your email domain isn't on the allowlist, and there's no pending invitation for this address. Ask an admin to invite you.
errors.SIGNUP_INVITE_ONLY: Signup is invite-only on this Munin instance. Ask an admin to send you an invitation.
errors.INVITATION_EMAIL_MISMATCH: This invitation is for a different email address.
errors.UNAUTHORIZED: You need to sign in to do that.
errors.FORBIDDEN: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.RATE_LIMITED: Too many requests. Please slow down and try again.
errors.QUOTA_EXCEEDED: You've hit a quota limit. Upgrade or wait for it to reset.
errors.VALIDATION_ERROR: The data you submitted didn't validate.
errors.FEEDBACK_RATE_LIMIT: Too many suggestions created in the last hour. Try again later.

terms.title: Terms of Use — Munin
terms.description: Terms governing use of getmunin.com and the Munin open-source project.
